questions.answer: 'The thrill of victory and the agony of defeat'

questions.question: In the 60s, where would you find the former New York attorney Oliver Wendell Douglas and his socialite wife Lisa?

questions.answer: In a hurry, or to do something immediately

questions.question: What duo was famous in the 60s for their surf-and-sand musical comedies, such as Beach Blanket Bingo?

questions.answer: Someone who spent a lot of time looking in the mirror

questions.answer: Andy Taylor, Barney Fife, Opie, Aunt Bee

questions.answer: "the luckiest man on the face of the earth."

questions.question: Name the singer and actress who broke color barriers in the late 60s as the first African-American female to star in a TV show, NBC's Julia.

Complete the second line to the Chordettes' 'Mr. Sandman.' 'Mr. Sandman, bring me a dream.'
questions.answer: "Make him the cutest that I've ever seen."

questions.question: In 1960, what sprinter overcame childhood polio to become the first American woman to win three gold medals in track and field in a single Olympics?

questions.answer: You are deserving of a set-down, verbally or physically; looking for trouble

questions.answer: Buddy Holly, Ritchie Valens, "The Big Bopper" J.P. Richardson

questions.question: In 1971, what character said, "You've got to ask yourself one question: 'Do I feel lucky?' Well, do ya, punk?"

questions.answer: What's your location or where are you?

questions.answer: Scapegoat; someone left to face consequences; a gullible person

questions.answer: They already knew something, or it was common knowledge
